evals.prompt: My storefront is showing $0.00 for all product prices. Everything else looks fine — products are loading, search works. Where do I start debugging?
evals.expected_output: Should narrow down where prices come from in an EDS + ACO setup: the Catalog Service resolves prices from the Price Book associated with the active catalog view. Diagnostic steps should cover at least 2 to 3 of: (a) inspect the Catalog Service request in DevTools Network tab to see whether prices are zero in the JSON response itself vs. just in the rendered UI; (b) verify Storefront Configuration headers.cs contains a valid AC-View-ID; (c) confirm in ACO admin that the catalog view has an associated Price Book and that the Price Book is populated; (d) check that price data has synced from Adobe Commerce to ACO via the Optimizer Connector or SaaS data export. Should not invent header names — any header named should match the documented names from the Adobe Commerce storefront configuration documentation.

evals.prompt: I'm setting up a brand new EDS storefront. I've forked the boilerplate and connected it to my DA site. What do I need to configure to get it talking to my ACO backend?
evals.expected_output: Should cover the documented setup for an EDS storefront talking to ACO: (1) the Storefront Configuration file is `config.json` in the repo (taking precedence when present) and is also managed at the EDS Config Service at `admin.hlx.page/config/{ORG}/sites/{SITE}/public.json` for production — the docs warn not to commit `config.json` to `main` for production sites. (2) Minimum documented config keys for ACO: `commerce-endpoint` (ACO GraphQL endpoint), `headers.cs.AC-View-ID` (catalog view ID, required), `analytics.environment-id` (tenant ID), `analytics.view-id`, and `analytics.base-currency-code`. `headers.cs.AC-Price-Book-ID` is documented as optional. (3) The configuration is cached by the EDS CDN with `max-age=7200` (2 hours), so changes take up to 2 hours to propagate. Should not invent config keys — any key cited should match documented names.

evals.prompt: I'm getting a 418 Configuration Error page when I load my storefront. The page just says 'Configuration Error'. How do I fix this?
evals.expected_output: Should walk through documented diagnostics for a Configuration Error on storefront load: (1) Clear stale config — the configuration is cached in browser `sessionStorage` and on the EDS CDN (`max-age=7200`, 2 hours); use DevTools > Application > Session Storage to clear, or test in an incognito window. (2) Validate the Storefront Configuration JSON is well-formed and contains the required keys for the active backend. (3) Verify the configured endpoints (e.g. `commerce-endpoint`) are reachable — a direct curl against the endpoint distinguishes a network/CORS failure from a config-parsing failure. (4) Inspect the browser console — startup errors from the storefront's initialization scripts surface there. Should not assert specific internal causes (e.g. "418 means X function threw") unless documented.

evals.prompt: I'm using the EDS boilerplate storefront with the Commerce drop-in components. How do I add a product recommendations unit to a category page? It's not a PDP so I'm not sure how to pass the context.
evals.expected_output: Should explain the DA block table structure for adding a product-recommendations block: block name in the first cell, recId row with the recommendation unit ID from ACO, optional currentSku row for context-aware types. Should clarify that currentSku is only needed on non-PDP pages when using context-aware types (e.g. Customers Also Viewed) — on a PDP the block auto-detects SKU from adobeDataLayer. Must NOT answer using a headless JS SDK approach (fabricating npm packages, SDK client classes, or .register()/.fetch()/.render() methods) — the correct EDS approach is the DA block + drop-in component, not a standalone JS SDK integration. Should fetch from the Storefront documentation for current block format and drop-in API details.

evals.prompt: How do I customize the layout of the cart drop-in? I want to move the order summary to the left side.
evals.expected_output: Should explain that the documented way to customize drop-in layout is the granular containers approach — rendering individual containers into a custom DOM structure rather than relying on the default monolithic container. A CSS ordering approach (using 'order' on wrapper columns) is also acceptable if the class names are cited from the boilerplate or fetched documentation, but the response must still mention the containers API as the primary supported customization surface. Must warn not to modify files in scripts/__dropins__/ directly — that directory is closed-source and will be overwritten on npm install. Must not invent container names or CSS class names — any specific names must come from fetched Storefront documentation or the boilerplate reference repo.

evals.prompt: What is the difference between commerce-endpoint and commerce-core-endpoint in Storefront Configuration? Do I need both?
evals.expected_output: Should clearly explain the two-endpoint split: commerce-endpoint is always ACO (Catalog Service) — handles search, PDP, recommendations, pricing. commerce-core-endpoint is Adobe Commerce (transactions) — handles cart, checkout, auth, B2B. Should note that commerce-core-endpoint is optional: if omitted, both fall back to commerce-endpoint, which means ACO handles everything (only valid if ACO supports those operations). Should reference the Storefront Configuration documentation for the full config key list.

evals.prompt: How do I create a new custom block for my EDS storefront? I want to build a featured-products block that shows a hardcoded list of SKUs.
evals.expected_output: Should explain the block structure: blocks/<name>/<name>.js exporting a default decorate(block) function, optional <name>.css. Should explain how to read block config from DA using readBlockConfig. Should note blocks are triggered by EDS based on section markup in DA. Should advise checking the Storefront documentation and the user's existing blocks/ directory for current block patterns and repo conventions.

evals.prompt: I've enabled B2B features on my Adobe Commerce backend — company accounts and purchase orders are working in the Admin. But on my EDS storefront, company login and the purchase order UI aren't showing up. What Storefront Configuration do I need to set?
evals.expected_output: Should explain that the EDS storefront has Storefront Configuration flags that gate B2B UI features in the drop-ins, separate from the backend module being enabled. Should cover: the B2B features must be explicitly enabled in Storefront Configuration with the documented config keys, and both the backend (B2B module enabled) and the storefront config flags must be set for the features to appear. Should fetch from the Storefront Configuration documentation before naming any specific config key names — must not invent flag names such as 'commerce-b2b-enabled' without confirming them from the docs. Should note that some B2B features also require the commerce-core-endpoint to be configured (pointing to the ACCS/PaaS instance, not just ACO).

evals.prompt: How do I add login and account features to my EDS storefront? I see there's a storefront-auth drop-in but I'm not sure how to set it up.
evals.expected_output: Should explain the storefront-auth drop-in installation and initialization pattern: (1) the drop-in is installed as an npm package and wired via the import map in head.html; (2) an initializer file in scripts/initializers/ configures the drop-in with the Commerce customer endpoint and any custom event handlers; (3) the auth drop-in provides sign-in, sign-up, forgot-password, and token management, and integrates with the commerce-core-endpoint for customer authentication. Should note that the auth drop-in is a prerequisite for account, order history, and B2B features. Should fetch from the Storefront drop-in documentation before naming specific initializer config keys, event names, or import alias values — must not invent these. Should advise reading scripts/initializers/ in the user's repo if available to see how other drop-ins are initialized as a reference pattern.

evals.prompt: My checkout drop-in just doesn't render — the page loads but the drop-in area is completely blank. There's no 418 Configuration Error and no obvious JavaScript errors in the console. Where do I look?
evals.expected_output: Should distinguish this from the 418 Configuration Error scenario (which has an explicit error page). Diagnostic steps should cover at least 3 of: (a) verify the import map in head.html has a correct alias for the checkout drop-in package pointing to the installed version in scripts/__dropins__/ — an incorrect alias means the module silently fails to load; (b) verify npm install was run and scripts/__dropins__/storefront-checkout/ (or equivalent) is populated with the drop-in source; (c) check that the block name in the DA page matches the expected block name exactly (EDS block lookup is case-sensitive and hyphen-normalized); (d) check that AEM Code Sync has deployed the latest code changes — the Admin API status endpoint shows whether the CDN copy is current; (e) check the browser Network tab for 404s on drop-in module fetches. Should fetch from the Storefront documentation and/or the boilerplate reference repo (hlxsites/aem-boilerplate-commerce) to confirm import map syntax and block naming conventions before recommending specific values. Must not invent module alias names or import map syntax.

evals.prompt: I need to set up catalog views and price books in Adobe Commerce Optimizer. Where do I start and what information will I need?
evals.expected_output: Should explain ACO catalog views (define which subset of catalog data is exposed) and price books (define pricing per customer group). Should cover: where to find the ACO admin UI, what a tenant ID is and where to find it, how catalog view ID and price book ID are used in Storefront Configuration headers (AC-View-ID, AC-Price-Book-ID). Should fetch from ACO overview docs for current UI steps.

evals.prompt: My products are not showing up in Live Search on my storefront. The catalog looks correct in the Adobe Commerce admin. What should I check?
evals.expected_output: Should identify the SaaS data export as the likely culprit — products must sync from Adobe Commerce to the Catalog Service before they appear in Live Search or ACO. Should explain the data export pipeline and how to check sync status. Should cover: verifying the SaaS data export module is installed and running, checking for sync errors, and how to trigger a manual re-index. Should note sync timing as a diagnostic: incremental changes take up to ~15 minutes, initial full sync can take 30–60+ minutes. Should fetch from saas-data-export docs.

evals.prompt: How do I enable B2B features like company accounts, shared catalogs, and purchase orders on my Adobe Commerce backend?
evals.expected_output: Should explain that B2B is a separate module/package that must be installed. Should cover: enabling B2B features in the admin (Stores > Configuration > B2B Features), company account creation, shared catalog setup, and purchase order workflow configuration. Should note that B2B features also require specific Storefront Configuration flags (commerce-b2b-enabled, commerce-companies-enabled) on the EDS storefront side. Should fetch from the B2B admin docs.

evals.prompt: Where do I find my ACO tenant ID, catalog view ID, and the GraphQL endpoint URL I need to put in my Storefront Configuration?
evals.expected_output: Should explain where each piece of information lives: tenant ID in the ACO admin or provisioning email, catalog view ID in ACO Catalog > Views, GraphQL endpoint format (na1.api.commerce.adobe.com/<TENANT_ID>/graphql or similar). Should direct to the ACO admin UI and relevant ExL Commerce docs. Should not invent specific URLs — fetch from ACO overview docs for current endpoint format.

evals.prompt: How do I set up Adobe Payment Services for my Commerce store? What does the onboarding process look like?
evals.expected_output: Should explain that Payment Services is a SaaS extension for Adobe Commerce that handles payment processing including credit/debit cards, PayPal, Apple Pay, and Google Pay. Should cover the onboarding steps: installing Payment Services from the Commerce Marketplace (Composer), enabling in Admin under Stores > Configuration > Sales > Payment Methods, completing the Adobe I/O connect and PayPal merchant onboarding, sandbox testing, and switching to production mode. Should note that Payment Services requires a valid Adobe Commerce license and that the sandbox/production modes are switched in the Admin configuration. Should fetch from the ExL Payment Services documentation. Must not invent Admin menu paths or configuration key names — any specifics cited must come from fetched documentation.

evals.prompt: I want to enable Product Recommendations on my Adobe Commerce store. What do I need to install and configure on the backend?
evals.expected_output: Should explain that Product Recommendations is a SaaS service powered by Adobe Sensei that requires: (1) installing the product-recommendations Composer module, (2) configuring SaaS data export to sync catalog and behavioral data to Adobe's services, and (3) configuring the Admin (Stores > Configuration) with the SaaS environment API keys. Should explain that behavioral data (browsing, purchases) powers the ML models and that the more data collected, the better the recommendations. Should distinguish backend setup (this question) from storefront rendering of recommendation units (handled by the drop-in/block). Should note that recommendation types like 'Most Viewed' or 'Trending' require sufficient behavioral data before they return results. Should fetch from ExL Product Recommendations documentation. Must not invent config key names or Admin paths.

evals.prompt: I'm deploying Adobe Commerce on Cloud (PaaS). My environment variables are set in the Cloud Console but my store isn't picking up the new values. How does environment configuration work on PaaS?
evals.expected_output: Should explain the PaaS configuration hierarchy: environment variables set in the Cloud Console override values in `app/etc/env.php`, but a redeployment (or at least a cache flush) is required for some changes to take effect — specifically, static environment variables defined in `.magento.app.yaml` require a redeploy, while Cloud-Console-only variables propagate without redeployment but may still require cache flush. Should explain that `app/etc/env.php` is generated during deployment from the cloud environment config and `.magento.app.yaml` relationships. Should cover how to verify active config values (`bin/magento config:show` or checking `env.php`). Should fetch from ExL PaaS cloud infrastructure guide. Must not invent specific CLI commands or config hierarchy details — fetch documentation to verify.

evals.prompt: I'm trying to access my DA site at admin.da.live but I get a 'Not permitted' error even though I'm logged in with my Adobe ID. What's going on?
evals.expected_output: Should distinguish authenticated (logged in) vs authorized (in the permissions sheet). Should instruct to check the IMS org switcher first — the user may be in the wrong Adobe org. Should explain the sheet-based ACL at da.live/config#/<org>/ and that access is granted via email addresses or IMS user groups. Should note that if no one has CONFIG write access, the user must contact Adobe support through their official support channel (not reference internal channels).

evals.prompt: How do I create a new content page for my EDS storefront using Document Authoring?
evals.expected_output: Should explain the DA content authoring workflow: create a new document in the DA site (da.live), structure it with sections and blocks, use the Sidekick browser extension to preview (aem.page) and publish (aem.live). Should explain that the URL path in DA maps to the URL on the storefront. Should fetch from the DA or AEM documentation for current step-by-step details.

evals.prompt: How do I add SEO metadata like a page title, description, and og:image to a content page in DA?
evals.expected_output: Should explain the Metadata block in DA — a table with 'Metadata' in the header cell and key-value rows for title, description, image, etc. Should explain how these map to HTML meta tags on the rendered page. Should fetch from the DA or AEM authoring documentation for the current metadata block format.

evals.prompt: I want to give a new team member access to author content in our DA site. How do I add them?
evals.expected_output: Should explain the sheet-based ACL at da.live/config#/<org>/. Should cover: someone with CONFIG write access must edit the permissions sheet, access can be granted by individual email or IMS user group, and the user must log in with an Adobe IMS-supported account. Should note users can authenticate via any IDP supported by Adobe IMS (not only Adobe ID). Should fetch from the DA or AEM documentation for the current permission sheet format.

evals.prompt: I have 200 product category pages and I want to set SEO metadata for all of them without editing each page individually. Is there a way to do this in bulk in Document Authoring?
evals.expected_output: Should explain the bulk metadata approach: a spreadsheet (typically at /metadata.xlsx or /metadata in the DA site) that maps URL path patterns to metadata key-value pairs such as title, description, and image. Should explain that the EDS framework applies this metadata at publish time without needing individual page edits. Should distinguish bulk metadata (spreadsheet-driven, pattern-matching) from page-level Metadata blocks (per-page table in DA). Should note that the bulk metadata sheet must be published via Sidekick to take effect. Should fetch from AEM/EDS documentation for the current bulk-metadata sheet format and path conventions. Must not invent spreadsheet column names or URL patterns.

evals.prompt: I want to use a single text string — like a phone number or promo message — in multiple pages across my DA site, so I only have to update it in one place. How do I do that?
evals.expected_output: Should explain the placeholders feature: a spreadsheet (typically at /placeholders.xlsx or accessible at /placeholders.json) that maps placeholder keys to their text values. Should explain that blocks and scripts in the EDS codebase can reference these keys (commonly as {{key}} in DA or via the fetchPlaceholders() utility in JS). Should explain that updating the spreadsheet and republishing propagates the change everywhere the placeholder is used. Should note this is an EDS feature documented in aem.live, not a DA-specific feature, so the agent should fetch from AEM/EDS docs for the current placeholder sheet format and JS utility. Must not invent key syntax or utility function names.

evals.prompt: I want to apply a custom CSS style to just one section of a page in DA, without affecting other sections. How do I do that?
evals.expected_output: Should explain section metadata in EDS/DA: adding a Metadata table directly after a section divider (---) in the DA document applies metadata only to that section. The 'style' key in the section metadata table maps to CSS class names added to the section wrapper element. Should explain that this is distinct from a page-level Metadata block (which applies to the whole page). Should note that the CSS classes added via 'style' must exist in the site's stylesheet. Should fetch from AEM/EDS documentation for the current section metadata format. Must not invent section metadata key names beyond what the docs confirm.
